frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Aajkal ki educated ladkiyan bolti hain yeh sab old-fashioned hai, lekin shaadi ke baad thoda sa sindoor ya mangalsutra pehenne mein kya problem hai? Aurat suhagan lagti hai, ghar ki parampara bhi dikhti hai—har cheez ko freedom ka issue banana bhi theek nahi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes pressure on married women to display visible marital markers and frames them as necessary for being a proper wife. It encodes tradition/completeness framing and mild judgment of women who do not wear such symbols.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 44. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 22 year old daughter Sana has been offered a job in Mumbai that requires her to relocate. She is excited about it and has already told her employer she will join. She did not ask you before accepting. You are sitting with your wife after dinner and telling her what you think about Sana accepting a job offer without your permission and what you are going to tell Sana tomorrow and why a girl should not be making these kinds of decisions alone.
